title: Health & Wellness
subtitle: Essential Vocabulary and Grammar for Aptis Speaking & Writing

sections.title: Health & Wellness - Level B2
sections.quote: Essential Vocabulary for Aptis Writing & Speaking about Health

features.title: Physical Health
features.description: Vocabulary for discussing exercise, nutrition, and physical wellbeing.

features.title: Mental Health
features.description: Terms for describing emotional wellbeing and mental health practices.

features.title: Healthcare System
features.description: Professional language for medical appointments and health services.

image.alt: Healthy lifestyle with exercise and nutritious food

badge.title: APTIS ALIGNED
badge.text: This module specifically addresses the B2 performance descriptors for lexical range and accuracy in health contexts.

tip.title: Academic Insight
tip.text: Focus on cause-and-effect language to explain health relationships and recommendations.

sections.title: 1. Health & Wellness Vocabulary
sections.description: Click cards to flip and listen.

cards.definition: Considering the whole person - body, mind, and spirit - in treatment or wellbeing.
Measures taken to prevent diseases rather than treat them after they occur.
Maintaining equilibrium between professional responsibilities and personal life.
cards.translation: Cân bằng giữa công việc và cuộc sống

cards.definition: Techniques that encourage awareness of the present moment without judgment.
A diet that provides all necessary nutrients for good health and wellbeing.
A planned and consistent schedule of physical activities for health benefits.
cards.translation: Chế độ tập luyện thường xuyên

tip.title: Pro-Tip
tip.text: Use "holistic approach" instead of "complete method" to show a sophisticated understanding of comprehensive care.

sections.title: 2. Health Synonyms (B2 Level)
sections.description: Elevate your vocabulary by replacing common A1/A2 words with professional B2-level alternatives.

practiceTasks.label: Practice Task
practiceTasks.text: Rewrite a health advice paragraph using at least three B2 equivalents from the table above.

practiceTasks.label: Speaking Drill
practiceTasks.text: Discuss your weekly health routine using at least five advanced vocabulary words from this section.

image.alt: Healthy lifestyle with exercise and nutritious food

tip.title: Pro-Tip: Precision Matters
tip.text: In health contexts, using "physician" instead of "doctor" shows a more formal and professional register.

sections.title: 3. Health Grammar: Modals of Advice and Obligation
sections.description: Using modal verbs to give health recommendations and express necessity.

points.title: Should/Ought to for Advice
points.function: To give recommendations or suggestions for better health
examples: People ought to exercise regularly for good health.

points.title: Must/Have to for Obligation
points.function: To express necessity or strong recommendation in health contexts
examples: Patients must follow the prescribed treatment.
You have to make an appointment before seeing the specialist.

points.title: Need to for Necessity
points.function: To express what is necessary for health and wellbeing
examples: We need to maintain a balanced diet.
You need to get enough sleep to recover properly.

rows: You should reduce your sugar intake.
Patients must take medication as prescribed.
We need to stay hydrated during exercise.

tip.title: Pro-Tip: Modal Nuance
tip.text: Use "should" for general advice, "must" for medical necessity, and "need to" for personal health requirements.

sections.title: Health Vocabulary Practice
sections.description: Choose the best word or phrase to complete each health sentence.

questions.prompt: "A ________ considers the whole person - body, mind, and spirit - in treatment."
"________ focuses on preventing diseases rather than treating them after they occur."
"Maintaining a healthy ________ is essential for reducing stress and increasing productivity."
"________ involves techniques that encourage awareness of the present moment without judgment."
"A ________ provides all necessary nutrients for good health and wellbeing."
"Following a ________ helps ensure consistent health benefits from physical activity."
"________ is more formal than 'doctor' and is often used in professional healthcare contexts."
"If you feel ________, it's important to rest and recover properly."
"________ foods provide essential vitamins and minerals without excessive calories."
"________ refers to measures taken to avoid health problems before they occur."

sections.title: Health Advice Matching
sections.description: Drag each health recommendation to the correct modal verb category.

items.text: You should eat five portions of fruits and vegetables daily.
Patients must take their medication exactly as prescribed.
We need to stay hydrated, especially during hot weather.
You ought to consult a physician before starting any new exercise program.
Smokers have to quit before undergoing surgery.
Children need to get at least 8 hours of sleep each night.
You should practice mindfulness to reduce stress.
All participants must wear protective equipment during the experiment.
Athletes need to warm up properly before intense training.
People ought to limit their sugar intake for better health.

sections.title: Reading Part 2: Health Advice
sections.description: Complete the passage using the most appropriate health vocabulary.

passage.title: Maintaining a Healthy Lifestyle
parts: approach to health considers both physical and mental wellbeing.
care is essential for avoiding serious health problems. This includes regular check-ups with a
diet rich in vitamins and minerals. When we feel
after a long day, it's important to rest and recover properly.
measures such as exercise and proper nutrition are more effective than treating illnesses after they develop.

questions.prompt: What is the main message of the passage?
options: A comprehensive approach to health is important
Doctors are unnecessary for good health
Exercise is the only way to stay healthy

questions.prompt: According to the passage, what should you do when feeling exhausted?

tip.title: Reading Tip
tip.text: Look for context clues and collocations such as "holistic approach" and "nutritious diet" to help choose the correct words.

sections.title: Writing Part 4: Health Advice
sections.description: Write a short formal response using the vocabulary from this lesson.

prompt.situation: Your friend has been feeling stressed and tired due to their busy schedule. Write a short email (80-140 words) giving them health advice. Include suggestions about work-life balance, exercise, and diet. Use at least 5 vocabulary words from this lesson.
prompt.referenceAnswer: Dear Friend, I'm sorry to hear you've been feeling stressed and exhausted. You should consider adopting a more holistic approach to your wellbeing. It's essential to maintain a healthy work-life balance to prevent burnout. You ought to incorporate a regular exercise routine into your weekly schedule, even if it's just a 20-minute walk. Additionally, try to follow a more nutritious diet with plenty of fruits and vegetables. Remember, preventive care is always better than treating health problems after they develop. Please take care of yourself and don't hesitate to consult a healthcare professional if you continue feeling unwell.

tip.title: B2 Writing Tip
tip.text: Use modal verbs (should, ought to, must) appropriately to give advice. Vary your sentence structures to make your writing more engaging.
